title: Network Connectivity
description: Network monitoring tools use Union-Find to efficiently track connectivity between hosts. When links go up or down, union and find operations determine in near-constant time whether two hosts can still reach each other, without running a full graph traversal.

title: Image Segmentation
description: Image segmentation algorithms group adjacent pixels with similar properties into regions using Union-Find. As the algorithm scans the image, it unions neighboring pixels that meet a similarity threshold, efficiently maintaining disjoint sets that represent distinct image segments.

title: Kruskal's MST
description: Kruskal's algorithm for minimum spanning trees sorts edges by weight and uses Union-Find to efficiently determine whether adding an edge would create a cycle. This enables network designers to build optimal backbone topologies connecting all nodes at minimum total cost.
